item.title: How two 'Origin plays' turned the tide for New South Wales against Queensland
item.description: With Queensland on the verge of another classic ambush, it took two "Origin plays" for New South Wales to turn the tide in the women's series opener in Newcastle.

item.title: Police use tear gas amid violent scenes outside Alice Springs Hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Alice Springs erupts in violence after 5yo girl's alleged killer arrested
item.description: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Police use tear gas amid violent scenes outside Alice Springs Hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Guy Montgomery went from lazy school student to hosting a TV spelling bee across two countries
item.description: Guy Montgomery is back with the third season of his inimitable Spelling Bee, with brand-new sidekick Sam Campbell. We turn the tables on the Kiwi comic to ask him the tough questions.

item.title: Seasonal produce for all your autumn soups, roasts and salads
item.description: From carrots to cauliflower, here is some of the best seasonal produce to enjoy in your autumn soups, roasts and salads.

item.title: Bond yields at crisis level as Australian government's 10-Year Treasury rate passes 5 per cent
item.description: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, pushes past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

item.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?
item.description: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: New photo of Aung San Suu Kyi released as military says she is moved from prison to house arrest
item.description: Myanmar's detained former president Aung San Suu Kyi has been moved from prison to house arrest, according to state media.

item.title: China using a double-insurance strategy to secure crude oil supplies amid Iran war
item.description: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Pacific nations aim to tackle regional unrest with expanded Pacific Response Group
item.description: The Pacific Response Group is beginning negotiations on a treaty that would allow it to respond more quickly to natural disasters and other crises across the region.

item.title: The algal bloom may be 'gone' but regional South Australians are grappling with a changing environment
item.description: While testing has shown a dramatic improvement in South Australia's algal bloom situation, industry and experts fear it won't be a quick recovery.

item.title: Once-safe Coalition seat Farrer now in sights of One Nation at by-election
item.description: A fascinating four-cornered contest is brewing as voters in the southern NSW seat of Farrer head to the ballot box for the second time in 12 months.

item.title: Mount Isa City Council moves to sell homes over unpaid rates in outback mining city
item.description: In Mount Isa, nearly 50 properties, some abandoned, have accrued years of unpaid rates and could be forced to sale.

item.title: At a Melbourne hospital, nitrous oxide users are presenting with psychosis
item.description: Harms from nitrous oxide have been growing in Australia in recent years, with the substance linked to a growing number of accidental deaths, while a study from a Melbourne hospital shows abuse of the inhalant is associated with psychosis.

item.title: Taylor says government is in 'denial' about situation in NT town camps — as it happened
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says the federal government is in "denial" about the situation in town camps following the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions led by miners, ANZ shares slump despite profit jump — as it happened
item.description: The ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions but ANZ's profit announcement drags down the banks.

item.title: Extreme heat the most deadly yet preventable natural disaster, NSW Net Zero Commission says
item.description: An agency set up by the NSW government just two years ago says extreme heat is the most deadly yet most preventable natural hazard facing Australians.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood smashed for 24 off one over as Shubman Gill fires in the IPL
item.description: Shubman Gill takes Australian quick Josh Hazlewood to task as Gujarat took down Bengaluru in the IPL.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

item.title: Sea creatures, Anzac Day and AI camera woes feature in top WA photo picks for April
item.description: From dawn services to record-breaking swims, here are our top photo picks for Western Australia in April.

item.title: Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, charged with rape
item.description: Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, has been charged with three counts of rape relating to alleged offending against a woman in her 30s at Burleigh Heads on March 30.

item.title: Remote patient monitoring technology saving time, travel and money for regional mums during pregnancy
item.description: Remote health technology is saving regional patients time and money, despite many Victorian hospitals still running on paper records.

item.title: Britney Spears charged with driving under influence of alcohol and drugs
item.description: Britney Spears has been charged in California with driving under the influence of drugs and alcohol.

item.title: Construction ends on $915 million Parramatta Powerhouse museum set to open this year
item.description: Four years after work started, the highly anticipated Western Sydney museum is ready for the installation of five exhibitions, including a major show about space exploration.

item.title: Adelaide showed how good LIV Golf could be, but it was built on a flawed premise
item.description: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

item.title: From Foo Fighters to Jessie Ware and Nine Inch Noize — these are April's best albums
item.description: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

item.title: Future of Northern Territory ambulance service in doubt as paramedics reach breaking point
item.description: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

item.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan
item.description: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

item.title: Victoria pledges to close legal 'loophole' to prevent attacks on paramedics
item.description: Ambulance Victoria and the paramedics union say some people who attack paramedics have escaped mandatory jail terms due to the law's definition of "on-duty".

item.title: A deadline for Trump to end the Iran war is looming. He may seek to get around it
item.description: A deadline for US President Donald Trump to end the Iran war is about to expire. Here is what to expect.

item.title: Poliovirus linked to overseas transmission detected in Perth wastewater
item.description: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

item.title: Tense moment at FIFA Congress as Gianni Infantino tries to get Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands
item.description: FIFA boss Gianni Infantino is criticised as he attempts to encourage Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands.

item.title: Ricky Stuart says it is time Craig Bellamy put himself first after the NRL coach's shock diagnosis
item.description: Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says Craig Bellamy needs to start putting himself first after the Storm revealed the coaching legend had been diagnosed with a neurological condition.

item.title: AFL score reviews under scrutiny with some coaches questioning the point
item.description: Nobody wants to see their team robbed by a missed officiating decision. But as scrutiny over video assistant refereeing or umpiring continues, some are wondering whether it's all worth it.

item.title: Thick smoke rises from oil facility in Russia after Ukraine strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: More IP, more content, more jobs: What we learned at Screen Forever 40
item.description: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

item.title: How NSW halfback Jesse Southwell, a future Brisbane hero, broke Queensland's hearts
item.description: In her final act as a Novocastrian before heading to Brisbane, Jesse Southwell kicked the first field goal of her life to deliver victory for New South Wales in Origin I.

item.title: Billions of NDIS funds lost to 'integrity leakage' last year, executive tells parliamentary hearing
item.description: About $3.7 billion of National Disability Insurance Scheme money was handed out inappropriately last financial year, the scheme's integrity chief has told a parliamentary hearing.

item.title: Mastermyne Crinum Operations fined $7m after 2021 on-site death of Graham Dawson
item.description: Graham Dawson, 62, was killed in a roof collapse on a central Queensland mining site in 2021. The District Court in Brisbane heard Mastermyne Crinum Operations was criminally negligent and the death was avoidable.

item.title: Outback WA shire demands trucks get off road after beloved teacher killed in crash
item.description: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

item.title: Suns coach Damien Hardwick fires back at Saints' Ross Lyon's AFL draft comment
item.description: Gold Coast coach Damien Hardwick provides a spirited response to St Kilda counterpart Ross Lyon's comment about the AFL national draft.

item.title: Supreme Court finds Perth man David Moussa not guilty of father's murder on insanity grounds
item.description: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

item.title: Review finds fire ant eradication can succeed if flaws in 'mistrust and misinformation' are fixed
item.description: An independent review finds fire ant eradication is still achievable, but major issues, including poor communication, public mistrust and strategy confusion, must be fixed first.

item.title: Beaches closed near Wollongong as whale carcass removed at Bellambi
item.description: The sperm whale carcass was towed from Era Beach in the Royal National Park to Bellambi Boat Ramp in Wollongong this afternoon.

item.title: Kalgoorlie couple who underwent IVF treatment in shock after welcoming quadruplets
item.description: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

item.title: Retiring ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker on mistakes, rehabilitation and compassion
item.description: Heart is not something anyone expects from the court system, but it was something outgoing ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker, who retired in March after 15 years in the job, aimed to portray.

item.title: Melbourne Storm lose seven in a row for the first time in club history
item.description: The Melbourne Storm have sunk to a historic low, losing seven consecutive NRL matches for the first time as the Dolphins powered to victory at a wet Lang Park in Brisbane.

item.title: Rebel Wilson defamation trial told Charlotte MacInnes was 'fudging' her version of producer incident
item.description: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

item.title: NT government criticised for snubbing youth justice and incarceration Senate inquiry
item.description: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

item.title: Planning and Environment Court approves Sheraton high-rise development for Hervey Bay
item.description: Activists appealed the local council's approval of the 18-storey resort on the grounds that it would endanger local wildlife. The community fears the court's green light for the development will permanently change their seaside town.

item.title: May temperature records fall across Victoria as unseasonably warm weather continues
item.description: Weather records fall across the state, as a spell of summer-like conditions tips into the first day of May.

item.title: Crows claim epic after two goals inside final minute as Freo downs Dogs
item.description: Adelaide claims a remarkable one-point victory over Port Adelaide after Hayden Young fires the Dockers to victory over the Bulldogs.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Chinese-owned Landbridge launches international legal action over forced sale of Port of Darwin
item.description: Anthony Albanese pledged at the federal election that Labor would return the port to Australian hands, saying it necessary for national security.

item.title: Six Australians among Gaza aid flotilla activists taken to Crete after Israeli interception
item.description: More than 100 activists, including six Australians, on ships bound for Gaza were intercepted by Israeli forces earlier this week.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's new proposal to end the war, threatening to 'blast them to hell' if deal not reached
item.description: Peace talks between the United States and Iran have stalled, with US President Donald Trump threatening to "blast them to hell" if they don't reach a deal.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of an iconic album cover, a shredding Banksy and that creepy dancing baby gif
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: A guide to the 2026 Stella Prize shortlist, featuring Geraldine Brooks, Tasma Walton and more
item.description: A memoir by Geraldine Brooks and a historical novel by actor-turned-author Tasma Walton are among the eclectic works up for one of Australia's most prestigious literary awards.

item.title: Deep fractures in the Gulf over Iran have implications for everyone, including Australia
item.description: Donald Trump's earlier signalling that he will neither walk away from the war declaring a victory or escalate it, but will try to starve the Iranians out, suggests there is no clear sign of how this war could end.

item.title: Iran and US play 'game of chicken' as regime's oil stockpiled inside blockade
item.description: Analysis of how much oil Iran is producing, shipping and having to store shows pressure is mounting on Tehran, but while experts agree the US blockade is effective, there are questions about whether it can really force Iran back to the negotiating table.

item.title: Gas vs beer: How David Pocock's 57-second video caused the government a weeks-long tax headache
item.description: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about the beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

item.title: What is a town camp? The site of Kumanjayi Little Baby's tragic alleged abduction
item.description: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

item.title: Secrecy, 'manipulation' and a long interest in guns: Sajid Akram's road to Bondi
item.description: Sajid Akram was a man of unusual secrecy — even to his own family. New details uncovered by Background Briefing shed light on his downward spiral into violence.

item.title: Albanese says US under Trump 'playing a different role' in world
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the United States is "playing a different role" in the world under President Donald Trump, forcing Australia and allies to adjust how they respond to unexpected conflicts.

item.title: Restrictions after drop in Menindee Lakes water level are ineffective, expert warns
item.description: A loophole in the NSW government's water restrictions in the northern basin of the Murray Darling means river water is still being sucked up by a handful of major licence holders.

item.title: Road safety advocates want better import standards for large utes
item.description: Sales of large utes have increased by 270 per cent since 2019, data from a national transport safety body.

item.title: Alternative Suspension breaks cycle for repeatedly suspended students
item.description: Of the students who have attended the Alternative Suspension program, 67 per cent have received no further suspensions.

item.title: Why Taiwan is becoming a hotspot for Australian travellers
item.description: Lots of Australians travel to Taiwan chasing flavour and wanting to try the best beef noodle soup — but they leave wanting to explore more of its history and nature.

item.title: Starc takes 3 wickets on IPL return to halt Delhi Capitals' losing run, Labuschagne leads Hyderabad to PSL final
item.description: Mitchell Starc picked up three wickets on his first bowl since January's BBL final, reviving Delhi Capitals, while Marnus Labuschagne led Hyderabad United to the PSL final.

item.title: SA Liberals poised to hold firm in upper house as Dennis Hood rejects presidency role
item.description: Labor's path to getting legislation through the upper house may get slightly more difficult after Liberal MLC Dennis Hood — who was strongly linked to the upper house presidency role — said he would remain on the floor of parliament.

item.title: Mornington Peninsula voters head to polls for Nepean by-election
item.description: The by-election has already had a high turnout of early voters, with the Victorian Electoral Commission saying about half the electorate had already voted by Friday night.

item.title: Iranian and FIFA football officials to meet ahead of World Cup games in USA
item.description: Iranian soccer officials are set to meet with FIFA in Zurich about their FIFA World Cup games in the United States in June, with Iran's football boss saying there are "many issues to discuss".

item.title: Australian cows help rebuild Indonesian dairy herds after foot-and-mouth outbreak
item.description: An Australian dairy program in Indonesia is helping farmers in West Java boost production after a devastating foot-and-mouth disease outbreak.

item.title: Australia's south-east basks in record May temperatures, but polar weather blast days away
item.description: Just weeks from winter, and south-east Australia is still enjoying summertime warmth.

item.title: 'Girl power movie', Bondi bathtub and Russian oligarch: Inside Rebel Wilson's defamation trial
item.description: Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson are locked in a messy defamation battle involving disparaging social media posts, a "Russian oligarch" and sexual harassment accusations.

item.title: Fifty years ago, music was on the brink of major change with seismic arrival of punk rock to centre stage
item.description: The music industry is always in a state of flux, but what was happening in 1976 was akin to the dinosaurs looking up and seeing a meteor on the way.

item.title: Why portrait painting still matters in the age of the selfie
item.description: What began as an awkward pose for the Archibald Prize became a meditation on why portrait painting still matters in the age of the iPhone.

item.title: Parents worried after dingoes spotted near Dampier Primary School
item.description: "Stressed-out" parents are wondering whether to let their children continue to get around unaccompanied, but wildlife advocates say the animals are not as threatening as their reputation suggests.

item.title: Canberra woman found guilty of culpable driving causing motorcyclist's death
item.description: The loved ones of a Canberra motorcyclist killed nearly two years ago say his death is a stark reminder that "full attention" behind the wheel can be the difference between life and death.

item.title: Number of Tasmania Police officers off duty due to mental health doubles in five years
item.description: More than 100 Tasmanian police officers are currently off duty due to mental health compensation claims, a number that has doubled in five years.

item.title: Removing the bund at Cape Palmerston National Park
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How salt water is restoring life to parts of the Queensland coast
item.description: More than half a century ago, lush wetlands along the Queensland coastline were cut off from the ocean as farmers built physical barriers to stop salt water flowing into their land. Now, conservation groups and traditional owners are restoring tidal flows and bringing life back to the land.

item.title: Restoring wetland habitat on cattle grazing property in Mackay
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia's fuel supply steady as US rejection of Iranian proposals casts doubts on peace deal — as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump says Iran is "asking for things that I can't agree to", but details of the proposal are unclear.

item.title: World Athletics president Sebastian Coe says super shoe technology should be enabled as records tumble
item.description: Lord Sebastian Coe says World Athletics has a duty to "enable" super shoe development despite concerns.

item.title: Jim Chalmers' productivity challenge | On Background
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Thousands travel for outback Agfair despite fuel costs
item.description: Fuel costs have not stopped thousands of people from descending upon Broken Hill for the biannual Agfair.

item.title: WA government 'confident' state has enough fuel for May as Middle East conflict drags on
item.description: WA's energy minister says the state will have the fuel it needs for this month and has "growing confidence" for June, as further details are released about 10 renewable energy projects that are set to be built across regional WA.

item.title: US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany amid Trump spat with Merz
item.description: The United States is withdrawing 5,000 troops from NATO ally Germany amid a growing a rift between the nations' leaders over the Iran war.

item.title: Ratko Mladić's lawyers say 'Butcher of Bosnia' near death and should be released
item.description: In a submission to a UN court, Ratko Mladić's lawyers say he has suffered a serious medical incident and should be released from The Hague on humanitarian grounds.

item.title: Meekatharra bike donation and workshop gets kids cycling to school
item.description: The Meekatharra community comes together to restore donated bikes, making a visible difference at the local school.

item.title: XPT retro revival, as iconic train returns to the tracks, after major refurbishment.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: This pumpkin soup is comfort food with a twist that takes it to the next level - ABC News
item.description: Pumpkin soup is one of a few meals with just one main ingredient. This delicious version spices things up with coconut cream and an easy curry-leaf oil.

item.title: Why Dee Salmin would rather be a 'feminist witch' than a 'cool girl'
item.description: Podcaster Dee Salmin says people should steer clear of traditional dating advice and focus more on themselves.

item.title: Conservation work helps endangered flora recover in Stirling Range
item.description: Seed collections taken before major bushfires in WA's Stirling Range National Park give hope for the continued survival of flora species found nowhere else in the world.

item.title: Australians likely to pay more for cosmetic items even if Strait of Hormuz reopens soon. Here's why
item.description: Australians spend about $450 on average on beauty products per year. That could soon rise as petroleum prices increase.

item.title: Carlton blows another half-time lead as Voss pins blame on defensive effort
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss has said his side's defence is not up to standard after the Blues suffered another second-half collapse on Saturday.

item.title: Storms, rare fogbows and spectacular sunsets — see WA's best weather photos for April
item.description: In classic shoulder season conditions, April delivered a transitional mix of warm beach weather and cool, wet days with rainfall in the double-digits as we inch closer to winter.

item.title: Student rocket team successfully launches Galah rocket 10,000 feet in NSW outback
item.description: Students with space industry aspirations successfully launch a rocket 10,000 feet in the air in regional Australia.

item.title: Sounding rocket Galah successfully launches at Tolarno Station.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: CCTV footage captures man attacking nun in Jerusalem
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Israeli police arrest man suspected of attacking nun near Jerusalem's Old City
item.description: Israeli police release a video showing what eyewitnesses say was an attack by a Jewish man on a nun near Jerusalem's Old City. Police say the suspect, a 36-year-old man, was later arrested.

item.title: Trump threatens to blast Iran to hell if they failed to strike a deal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Emotional scenes as Rooster returns after 1,337 days out with illness
item.description: The Roosters hang on for a 38-24 win over the Broncos, as Cody Ramsey plays his first NRL game in almost four years.

item.title: WA's energy minister sits down for a Stateline interview to discuss the state's fuel supply.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Spirit of Tasmania V arrives in Geelong as preparation for Bass Strait voyages continues
item.description: Following a seven-week journey from Scotland, the Spirit of Tasmania V has arrived in Geelong. It will undergo gantry testing tomorrow before being temporarily relocated to Station Pier in Melbourne.

item.title: Charges yet to be laid as Kumanjayi Little Baby's family thanks community for support
item.description: The family of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs on Thursday, has thanked the community for their support as tributes flow in from across the country.

item.title: Counting underway in Nepean by-election, with Liberal Party looking to retain Victorian state seat
item.description: The Liberal Party seeks to retain the seat vacated by former Victorian deputy Liberal leader Sam Groth.

item.title: Brisbane Lions coach Chris Fagan slams 'hero' parent after Essendon whiteboard leak
item.description: Chris Fagan has expressed his disappointment after the leaking of confidential Brisbane Lions notes on Essendon players.

item.title: Anthony Marsh wins Nepean by-election, retaining seat for Liberal Party
item.description: Anthony Marsh wins the Nepean by-election, retaining the seat for the Liberal Party.

item.title: Quiz: Welcome to your Sunday serve of Guy Montgomery's spelling stumpers
item.description: You lot take your spelling seriously. Repeat after me: It's just an internet quiz. It's just an internet quiz.

item.title: Trucking up the Hume as diesel prices boom
item.description: On Australia's busiest freight route, it takes four shots of coffee, $2,553 of fuel and a little hair metal to keep the wheels of the economy turning.

item.title: Radio star James Valentine shares what he learnt in his 'year of living gratefully'
item.description: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

item.title: Scam centre survivors criticise Steam video games that closely depict real-life compounds
item.description: Scam centre survivors say they are hurt by games that serialise scam compound experiences online, but one creator says he hopes people find the game "fun".

item.title: Mamdani says he would encourage King Charles to return Koh‑i‑Noor diamond
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: King Charles won over Donald Trump with his US visit but not progressive mayor Zohran Mamdani
item.description: King Charles's US state visit hit a bump in New York when Mayor Zohran Mamdani said if the pair were to speak, he would "probably encourage him to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond".

item.title: Australian taxpayers also take on risk for major oil and gas projects. Why is that ignored?
item.description: Australian governments take on risk to get major resource projects up and running, so why don't they take an equity stake?

item.title: The year that changed the shape of Albanese's agenda
item.description: The prime minister's second term is now defined less by what he wants to do than by what unforeseen events will allow.

item.title: What are we losing when big business buys up our favourite little pubs?
item.description: From a tiny Riverina pub to a polished inner-city institution, Australia's local pubs are changing hands at record pace. As big money pours in, what happens to the communities that built them?

item.title: Robert Forster from The Go-Betweens on his debut novel, Songwriters on the Run
item.description: The Go-Betweens frontman spent years developing the story, which follows a pair of musicians on the run from the law as they try to clear their names.

item.title: Physical and mental wellbeing program seeks to help veterans adjust to life after military service
item.description: When training and physical fitness are a part of the job, sometimes veterans can drop off when they discharge, to the detriment of their mental and physical wellbeing.

item.title: Police and Porepunkah come together to pick grapes in memory of officers killed by Dezi Freeman
item.description: The Porepunkah community and Victoria Police pick grapes together to make wine in honour of Neal Thompson and Vadim De Waart-Hottart, who were killed by Dezi Freeman.

item.title: Republicans concerned about NATO alliance after decision to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany
item.description: Two Republicans, who hold positions on armed services committees, say they have concerns for NATO alliances and security after the Pentagon confirms the withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany.

item.title: Family of 95yo Clare Nowland, who died after being tasered, hope inquest will lead to change
item.description: In an in-depth interview ahead of the inquest into his mother Clare Nowland's nursing home death, Mick says the family wants changes that will prevent another tragedy.

item.title: Mixed bag for Socceroos World Cup bolters, as Auckland FC, Sydney FC move to A-League semifinals
item.description: Socceroos hopeful Marcus Younis sees his season come to an end but a host of Australian young guns progress after an enthralling pair of A-League finals.

item.title: What a vote of confidence in New Zealand's PM could mean for the country's election
item.description: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon has survived a vote of confidence in his leadership amid a drop in approval ratings. Here's what this could mean for NZ's upcoming election.

item.title: Beck prepares for first Australian orchestra tour
item.description: The alt-rock anomaly has toured Australia numerous times over the past 30 years. But this month is his first accompanied by symphonic orchestras.

item.title: Australian activist Zack Schofield says crew of Gaza-bound aid flotilla was beaten by Israeli sailors
item.description: Zack Schofield was one of three Australians taken to hospital after being released by Israeli authorities, and says he saw at least 10 of his crew being shot by rubber bullets.

item.title: Man accused of murdering parents and brother in 'grisly' attack
item.description: A father, mother and brother have died after a family member allegedly assaulted them at a Rosemeadow home in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: F1 qualifying results: Oscar Piastri second in Miami Grand Prix sprint race, but qualifies seventh for main event
item.description: McLaren finally halts the Mercedes dominance with a one-two finish in the Miami sprint race, before a difficult qualifying session.

item.title: Women's and junior soccer players in limbo as SA club told men's team needed
item.description: Millicent United's women's side is flying this season, but without a men's team the club has been told they are unable to compete.

item.title: Kate Ceberano, Gurrumul, Spiderbait among 2026 ARIA Hall of Fame inductees
item.description: For the first time in more than a decade, multiple artists are being inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame.

item.title: Ryan Reynolds 'gutted' as Wrexham misses shot at Premier League promotion
item.description: Movie star and Wrexham co-owner Ryan Reynolds says he is "gutted" but proud after the Welsh club narrowly missed a shot at a Premier League promotion.

item.title: As Labor’s reign continues, the Liberals need to get creative. But are the Greens the answer?
item.description: In many ways, the Liberals and the Greens are complete opposites — but in the ACT, an Australian-first power-sharing arrangement between the two parties almost became a reality. So, in politics, can opposites really attract?

item.title: How Australia’s most iconic inland fish species has bounced back
item.description: Murray cod numbers have bounced back across much of Victoria and NSW, and that's good news for the whole Murray-Darling Basin ecosystem.

item.title: Liberals focus on 'lessons' for Victorian election after decisive Nepean by-election win
item.description: It took less than three hours from the start of vote counting for election analysts to call the result in the Liberals' favour, with a win for former local mayor Anthony Marsh.

item.title: Marta Kostyuk backflips as the Ukrainian defeats Russian Mirra Andreeva in Madrid Open final
item.description: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk backflips after beating Russian Mirra Andreeva in the Madrid Open women's final.

item.title: Trump reviews Iran peace proposal as speculation turns to strikes resuming — as it happened
item.description: The US president told reporters he was reviewing a new peace proposal from Iranian negotiators, but immediately cast doubt on whether it would be "acceptable".

item.title: Police charge Jefferson Lewis, 47, with 5yo girl's murder
item.description: Northern Territory Police have charged Jefferson Lewis with the murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs.

item.title: Liberals claim victory for Jo Palmer, tight race shaping in Huon following Legislative Council elections
item.description: Voters in Rosevears have given Education Minister Jo Palmer a strong lead, while the race in the southern electorate of Huon is much closer with independent Dean Harriss fighting to retain his seat.

item.title: Why leucism is the explanation for this orange and black, red-tailed black cockatoo in WA's South West
item.description: Spotted again in WA's South West, a rare condition is the reason for the bird's distinctive orange colouring.

item.title: WA's delays on Working with Children Check, child safety changes criticised
item.description: Western Australia is the only state to not recognise Working with Children Check rejections from around the country, and has been "slow" to make other child safety law changes, expert says.

item.title: After Kumanjayi Little Baby's death, Coalition demands government re-engage on Indigenous debate
item.description: Nationals leader Matthew Canavan says after the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the government must "move on" from its defeat at the Voice referendum and re-engage on Indigenous policy debate.

item.title: Talking Pictures With Mike Bowers and Tom Ballard
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Socceroos attacker Mohamed Toure scores again for Norwich City to conclude three-month campaign
item.description: Mohamed Toure scores his 10th goal in just 12 matches for Norwich to keep him in red hot form ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: Untold stories of women at Albany's whaling station inspire local artist
item.description: The overlooked contributions of women in the whaling industry are the subject of an exhibition in the Great Southern region of Western Australia.

item.title: Love story: When life changed in an instant, Lucy was Beau's rock
item.description: The goalposts moved dramatically on Beau and Lucy Vernon's life plans, but it brought them closer together.

item.title: This Australian medical first from The Alfred saved US marine Travis Reyes's life after fatal military aircraft crash
item.description: Travis Reyes survived a horror military aircraft crash but what came after was a two-year battle from doctors across Australia and the US to save his life.

item.title: Trundle ABBA Festival returns to transform farming community after break
item.description: For one day each year, the town of 335 people swaps tractors for tribute acts, grain trucks for glitter and everyday life for an all-out ABBA extravaganza.

item.title: Miami F1 Grand Prix start time moved three hours earlier due to predicted storms
item.description: F1's governing body announces the Miami Grand Prix will start three hours early, as fears grow about torrential rain.

item.title: Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt win gold at world mixed pairs curling championships
item.description: Australia, despite not having a dedicated curling facility, has claimed a first ever world championship title, with Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt victorious in Switzerland overnight.

item.title: Trump says resuming strikes on Iran 'a possibility'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: State-owned land to be unlocked for oil storage and refineries under new Queensland government program
item.description: The state government has announced the Accelerating Fuel Infrastructure Program, which it says is intended to ease fuel costs and protect against future price shocks.

item.title: Marshall slams 'unacceptable' Tigers after horror second stanza
item.description: Benji Marshall said he could not recognise his Wests Tigers side after a humiliating second half saw Cronulla dominate at Shark Park, while there were also wins for Newcastle and Penrith on a bumper Sunday of NRL football.

item.title: CCTV footage allegedly shows people looting a service station on Bagot Street in Alice Springs last week.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jamarra sparks Suns as teammate floors Oliver with bizarre face slap
item.description: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan makes his mark in the second half as tempers flare between Ben Long and Clayton Oliver in the fourth quarter.

item.title: Nepean by-election proof November state election will be tough contest for major parties
item.description: The Nepean by-election proves Victoria's election in November is likely to be tough for the two major parties, Richard Willingham writes.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese readies federal budget to favour gen Z and millennial voters
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is preparing to break an election commitment and overhaul property tax perks in this month's budget, to give younger Australians a "fair crack" at owning their own homes.

item.title: What’s driving US market record highs despite oil crisis?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Maria Psillakis, wife of shark victim, speaks on new drone program
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran peace proposal, Iran military warns war is 'likely'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Albanese to overhaul property tax breaks in upcoming budget.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iran ready for 'renewed' hostilities as Trump cools on peace proposal
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he will review a new Iranian peace proposal, but casts doubt over its prospects and leaves open the possibility of future attacks on Iran.

item.title: Two US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
item.description: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members who went missing in mountainous terrain.

item.title: Piastri makes late charge for podium as Leclerc is handed post-race penalty
item.description: Oscar Piastri says the future is exciting for McLaren after the Australian made a late charge to finish third in the Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix.

item.title: What can you use your personal leave for?
item.description: People employed on a permanent basis in Australia are entitled to 10 days' paid personal leave a year to help navigate illness, caring responsibilities, or family emergencies. But what are the different types of personal leave and when can you use them?

item.title: This week in history quiz, May 4: Can you name the typo on the $50 note?
item.description: Take a trip back in time with this week's history quiz.

item.title: Chargeback 'fraud' crippling small online businesses grappling with higher inflation
item.description: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

item.title: Australian police officers can be tracked due to a security flaw in tasers and body-worn cameras
item.description: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

item.title: Mariana's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin
item.description: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.

item.title: News avoidance on the rise as young people feel fear, anger and powerlessness about world events
item.description: The news has never been more accessible, yet people are tuning out at record levels. Here is how to strike a balance between keeping informed and looking after yourself.

item.title: The seven factors that will decide if the RBA raises interest rates on Tuesday
item.description: Business journalist Emily Stewart walks you through the elements that will contribute to the RBA's decision to change the cash rate on Tuesday.

item.title: The automation arms race will have huge consequences for workers
item.description: Artificial intelligence might be driving growth for data centre companies, but society is unprepared for the long-term risks posed to the rest of us.

item.title: One Nation's rise could be more than a temporary protest, new polling says
item.description: A new poll asks Australians specifically about One Nation and the results show that rather than being a flash in the pan, the minor party's freight train may be a more lasting phenomenon.

item.title: Treasurer to bank tax windfall from Iran war in federal budget
item.description: The extra tax revenue generated by the Iran war will be saved in its entirety, as the government downplays major cost-of-living relief in next week's federal budget.

item.title: Liveable Victoria launched to campaign against Labor's planning reforms
item.description: Residents' groups across Victoria have formed an alliance ahead of the state election, calling for the community and councils to be given a voice when it comes to approving high-density housing.

item.title: Revealed exhibition brings Aboriginal art from every corner of WA to the public
item.description: Revealed, Perth's long-running showcase of Aboriginal art from around Western Australia, allows budding artists to discover their creative side while strengthening their cultural bonds.

item.title: Bondi royal commission public hearings begin, with witnesses to share antisemitism lived experience
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will hear from people with lived experience in public sessions beginning in Sydney this week.

item.title: Queensland Rail is changing how it staffs train stations. Here's what you need to know
item.description: Queensland Rail says it is moving to a system where staffing is based on "customer needs and patronage", but the changes have been labelled as "extreme" that will make some stations less safe.

item.title: Dana Wing says her family is experiencing the health effects of mould inundation in their public housing property in Tasmania.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tasmanian public housing tenants in unlivable and unhealthy conditions, and needing help from regulator
item.description: Despite unsafe and unusable bathrooms, cockroach infestations eating through wiring and constantly damp carpets, some Tasmanian public housing tenants are having to get the regulator to intervene in order to get solutions.

item.title: Essential services and cultural events scaled back in remote communities due to cost of fuel
item.description: Cultural obligations and essential services are being impacted by the cost of fuel in Australia's most remote communities.

item.title: Community fears West Gippsland hospital at Drouin will not meet 2028 target
item.description: Victoria's fast-growing West Gippsland region was promised a new hospital by 2028, but work has not started.

item.title: Future of National Trust of South Australia uncertain as ousted members demand parliamentary inquiry
item.description: The National Trust of South Australia admits it is cash-poor and will need to improve its finances, amid disagreements with its regionally-based volunteers.

item.title: Crinum mine former senior executives to face court over fatal roof collapse
item.description: Two former bosses are facing charges over a roof collapse at an underground mine in central Queensland in 2021 that killed a worker and seriously injured another.

item.title: How people power is helping to restore bus routes across Sydney
item.description: Communities fighting to have their local bus or ferry routes restored should campaign hard and "stick it out for the long term", according to these residents in Pyrmont.

item.title: Ex-Man United manager Alex Ferguson hospitalised after feeling unwell at Premier League match
item.description: Sir Alex Ferguson is sent to hospital after feeling unwell ahead of his former club's Premier League match against Liverpool.

item.title: ACT government locks in direct light rail Stage 2B route to Woden following feedback
item.description: The ACT government sought feedback on two possible routes for light rail from the city south to Woden, and is now pushing ahead with its preferred more-direct path.

item.title: E-bike sales surge 300 per cent in Darwin as more people ditch cars and costly fuel
item.description: Bike shops in Darwin are experiencing soaring demand as more people turn to one of the oldest and simplest alternatives to cars amid skyrocketing fuel prices, but advocates say the federal government is still prioritising drivers.

item.title: Cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz reports being attacked as peace negotiations continue
item.description: A cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz says it has been attacked by multiple small craft, marking at least two dozen attacks in and around the strait since the Iran war began.

item.title: Live: ASX set to open lower, Wall Street hits new record, oil slips on Strait of Hormuz hope
item.description: The ASX is priced to fall this morning, despite strong corporate earnings propelling the S&P 500 to a new record close. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Iran briefing: Details on Iran's 14-point peace plan, a shift in Gulf power politics and eyes back on Gaza
item.description: Iran gives the US a 30-day deadline on its counteroffer for peace, which does not mention its nuclear ambitions.

item.title: Students use makeshift stretcher to rescue hiker in Warrumbungle National Park
item.description: A group of high school students use a makeshift stretcher and put their Duke of Edinburgh Award training to the test while rescuing an injured hiker.

item.title: Live: Japan's PM touches down in Canberra for first visit to Australia
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi has touched down in Canberra for her first visit to Australia. Follow live.

item.title: Sam Kerr becomes Chelsea's all-time leading WSL scorer in 3-1 win over Leicester
item.description: Sam Kerr creates history in Chelsea's 3-1 win at Leicester, as the Blues secure a berth in next season's Champions League.

item.title: Coastal council pushes for levy on fossil fuel giants to fund disaster recovery
item.description: Councillors in a Victorian shire battling coastal erosion say fossil fuel giants should help pay for damage caused by climate-related environmental disasters.

item.title: Residents say removal of sperm whale at Bellambi put them at risk of shark attack
item.description: Swimmers and surfers say they were given no warning before a 25-tonne whale carcass was towed past more than a dozen South Coast beaches, attracting sharks in its wake.

item.title: As voting opens in the ABC Classic 100: Greatest of All Time, we explore how classical music is thriving
item.description: As Australians vote for the greatest classical music of all time, we look at who is listening, how classical music is evolving, and how it fills the world around us.
